notes: Tasks '1214711861828802' (multiple status labels) and '1214703633094113' (two tags for Overstocked/Surplus) were ALREADY IMPLEMENTED today via the StatusBadges component in Badge.tsx. They are included here for reference only and require NO further coding.
The three remaining tasks below require implementation.

already_completed.title: FEATURE: Multiple status labels — show all qualified statuses for SKUs
already_completed.what_was_done: New StatusBadges component added to src/components/ui/Badge.tsx. Exports deriveStatusLabels(record) which returns an array of applicable badge descriptors. Logic: if status='Excess stock' AND on_order>0, shows both 'Excess Stock' and 'Surplus Orders' badges. If status='Surplus orders' AND excess_units>0, shows both. Used in InventoryBrowser.tsx status column and all four status cells in ActionCenter.tsx.

already_completed.title: Feature: Two tags for status Over-stocked and Surplus Order
already_completed.what_was_done: Same implementation as above. 'Over-stocked' = 'Excess stock' in the source data vocabulary.

codebase_context.stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, Supabase (Postgres), TanStack Query v5, Tailwind CSS, Recharts, lucide-react
key_conventions: All inventory data flows through useInventoryAnalysis() → analyzeInventory(). Never fetch records independently in page components.
Supabase queries always paginate: use .range(from, from + PAGE_SIZE - 1) in a loop.
Export to Excel uses downloadCsv() + inventoryToExportRows() from src/lib/exportCsv.ts. No xlsx dependency — Excel opens CSV natively.
Status values are exactly: 'Ok', 'Excess stock', 'Surplus orders', 'Potential s/o', 'Stocked out', 'New item' — case-sensitive.
At-risk = 'Potential s/o' OR 'Stocked out'. Excess = 'Excess stock' OR 'Surplus orders'.
Tailwind utility classes use shorthand var references: bg-surface2, text-danger, text-accent, text-warning, text-success, text-text1, text-text2, bg-border.
Sort state pattern: { field: string; dir: 'asc' | 'desc' }. SortableTh component renders clickable header with arrow icon.
All async mutations use try/catch/finally so loading state always clears.
atRiskByVendor is a Record<vendorName, InventoryRecord[]> used to pass vendor-scoped at-risk SKUs to TaskModal.

key_files.src/types/index.ts: InventoryRecord interface — all 36 fields including on_order, excess_units, excess_value, unsatisfied_customer_orders_units, back_orders, recommended_order, recommended_order_value
key_files.src/hooks/useInventory.ts: analyzeInventory() pure function, useInventoryAnalysis(), useAtRiskItems(), useExcessItems(), useBackorderItems()
key_files.src/lib/exportCsv.ts: downloadCsv(rows, filename), inventoryToExportRows(records) — covers all InventoryRecord fields
key_files.src/lib/utils.ts: fmtNumber(), fmtCurrency(), groupBy(), isOverdue(), estimatedArrivalMonth(), parseMonthLabel()
key_files.src/components/ui/Badge.tsx: Badge, statusVariant(), StatusBadges, deriveStatusLabels() — StatusBadges takes {record: InventoryRecord} and renders 1-2 status badges
key_files.src/components/tasks/TaskModal.tsx: Mode='single'|'vendor'. Vendor mode pre-loads SKUs via prefillVendorSkus and shows multi-SKU selector. atRiskByVendor drives vendor dropdown.
key_files.src/pages/purchasing/ActionCenter.tsx: At-risk table, Backorders table, Overstock section (with-orders + no-orders sub-sections). openTaskForSku(record) and openVendorTask(vendor, records) functions
key_files.src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.tsx: Vendor list table + expandable per-vendor SKU sub-table. Uses VendorView.helpers.ts
getVendorSkuRows(records, query, sort), getVendorViewAtRiskSkus(records), VendorSkuSortState type

tasks.title: FEATURE: Vendor View — expanded SKU sub-table needs status filter and badge display

current_state.description: VendorView.tsx shows a list of vendor rows. Clicking any vendor row expands an inline SKU sub-table for that vendor. The sub-table already has: (1) a text search input that filters by product_code, description, category_name, and status string, and (2) column sorting for product_code, description, category_name, on_hand, days_on_hand, recommended_order, status. However the expanded sub-table is MISSING a dedicated status filter dropdown, making it hard to isolate e.g. only at-risk SKUs within a large vendor. Also, the status column in the expanded sub-table renders raw text with conditional class coloring — it does not use the new StatusBadges component, so dual-badge SKUs display incorrectly.

changes_required.change: Add a status filter dropdown to the expanded vendor SKU panel header bar (alongside the existing text search input).
details: Add state: const [expandedSkuStatus, setExpandedSkuStatus] = useState<string>('') — reset to '' whenever expandedVendor changes (add to the existing useEffect that resets expandedSkuSearch and expandedSkuSort).

Render a <select> dropdown next to the text search inside the expanded panel. Options: '' (All statuses), 'Potential s/o', 'Stocked out', 'Excess stock', 'Surplus orders', 'Ok', 'New item'. Use the exact string values from InventoryStatus type.

Pass expandedSkuStatus as a new 4th argument to getVendorSkuRows() OR apply the status filter before passing records: filter rows where expandedSkuStatus === '' OR r.status === expandedSkuStatus, then pass filtered records to getVendorSkuRows().
The SKU count displayed in the top-right of the panel (e.g. '12 SKUs') must reflect the filtered count, not the total vendor SKU count.
Add a 'Clear' button that appears when either expandedSkuSearch or expandedSkuStatus is set, resetting both to ''.
The status dropdown should use the same .select CSS class as other selects in the app.

changes_required.change: Replace raw status text in the expanded SKU sub-table status column with the StatusBadges component.
details: Import StatusBadges from '@/components/ui/Badge'.
In the expanded table row (line ~378-384), replace the {r.status} element with .
This ensures dual-badge SKUs (e.g. Excess Stock + Surplus Orders) display both badges inline, consistent with InventoryBrowser and ActionCenter.

tasks.desired_outcome: When a vendor row is expanded in Vendor View, the SKU sub-table has both a text search AND a status filter dropdown. Selecting 'Potential s/o' shows only at-risk SKUs for that vendor. The status column renders StatusBadges for visual consistency with the rest of the app. The SKU count updates dynamically based on active filters.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking on a vendor with 50+ SKUs and selecting status='Potential s/o' shows only at-risk SKUs within that vendor.
Selecting status='Excess stock' shows only excess-stock SKUs.
Clearing the status filter (selecting 'All statuses') returns all SKUs.
The SKU count label (e.g. '3 SKUs') updates whenever either filter changes.
The Clear button appears whenever either text search or status filter is active, and resets both on click.
Any SKU qualifying for dual badges (e.g. Surplus orders + physical excess) shows two badges in the status column.
When the expanded vendor changes (clicking a different vendor row), both filters reset to empty.
Sorting still works independently of the status filter — applying a status filter and then clicking the Days OH column sorts within the filtered set.
out_of_scope: Adding export to the expanded sub-table (covered by TASK-EXPORT).
Pagination within the expanded sub-table — all matching SKUs for a vendor are shown.
Changing the VendorSkuSortState type — status can already be sorted, no change needed.

tasks.title: FEATURE: Add missing Excel export buttons to Action Center tables

current_state.description: ActionCenter.tsx currently has one Export button — on the Attention Required (at-risk) section, exporting visibleAtRisk rows. The following tables have NO export: (1) Open Backorders, (2) Overstock 'Open orders exist' sub-table, (3) Overstock 'No inbound orders' sub-table. VendorView.tsx also has no export for either the main vendor list or the expanded SKU sub-table, but the Asana task scope is explicitly Action Center.
current_state.existing_export_pattern: downloadCsv(inventoryToExportRows(someRecordArray), 'filename_YYYY-MM-DD.csv'). inventoryToExportRows() covers all 36 InventoryRecord fields. downloadCsv() triggers a browser file download. Both are imported from '@/lib/exportCsv'. The Download lucide icon is already imported in ActionCenter.tsx.

changes_required.change: Add Export button to the Open Backorders section header.
details: The backorders section header already has a Filter toggle button. Add an Export button immediately to the right of it, using the same pattern as the at-risk export: <button onClick={() => downloadCsv(inventoryToExportRows(visibleBackorders), `backorders_${new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)}.csv`)} className='btn-ghost text-xs flex items-center gap-1' title='Export backorder items to Excel'><Download size={12} /> Export</button>.
Use visibleBackorders (the already-sorted, already-filtered slice up to 50) as the export source, consistent with how at-risk export uses visibleAtRisk.
Filename pattern: backorders_YYYY-MM-DD.csv

changes_required.change: Add Export button to the Overstock section header (exports both sub-sections combined).
details: The Overstock section header has Filter + Show snoozed buttons. Add an Export button that exports ALL visible overstock items (both sub-sections combined): visibleOverstock.
onClick: downloadCsv(inventoryToExportRows(visibleOverstock), `overstock_${new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)}.csv`).
Exporting the combined set (not separately per sub-section) is intentional — managers will filter in Excel. One button per section is the UX pattern.
Filename pattern: overstock_YYYY-MM-DD.csv
Place the Export button in the flex gap-2 div alongside the existing Filter and Show snoozed buttons.

tasks.desired_outcome: Every action table in Action Center has a working Export button. Clicking Export on any table downloads a .csv file that Excel opens natively. The exported file contains all visible/filtered rows at the time of click, with all InventoryRecord fields as columns.
acceptance_criteria: The Open Backorders section header shows an Export button with Download icon and 'Export' label.
Clicking Backorders Export downloads a file named backorders_YYYY-MM-DD.csv containing the currently visible backorder rows (up to 50, respecting vendor/category filters).
The Overstock Actions section header shows an Export button.
Clicking Overstock Export downloads overstock_YYYY-MM-DD.csv containing all currently visible overstock rows from both sub-sections (open orders + no orders combined, up to 100).
Exported files open correctly in Excel with one header row and one data row per SKU.
If vendor or category filter is active in Backorders or Overstock, the export reflects the filtered rows, not all rows.
The Export button is visually consistent with the existing at-risk Export button (same btn-ghost class, same Download icon, same size).
Exporting when there are 0 visible rows is a no-op (downloadCsv already handles empty array).
out_of_scope: Adding export to VendorView (not in the Asana scope for this task).
Adding export to Inbound Pipeline (separate task if needed).
Separate export buttons per overstock sub-section — one button for both is sufficient.
Custom column selection or formatting in the export — inventoryToExportRows() covers all fields.

tasks.title: FEATURE: Task creation should default to vendor level with multi-SKU selection, not individual SKU level

current_state.description: TaskModal.tsx supports two modes — 'single' (one SKU) and 'vendor' (vendor order with multi-SKU selection). However, all row-level Task buttons in ActionCenter call openTaskForSku(record) which passes prefillSku=record.product_code and NO prefillVendor/prefillVendorSkus, so the modal always opens in 'single' mode. The only way to reach vendor mode from ActionCenter is via the Filter bar's 'Vendor Task' button (only visible when a vendor filter is active) or from VendorView. The Asana complaint is that task creation is still effectively per-SKU because the primary entry point (row-level Task button) is single-SKU. Additionally, vendor header rows in the grouped at-risk table have no Task button at all — only the vendor/category summary text is shown.

existing_logic.openTaskForSku: Sets prefillRecord=record, prefillVendor=undefined, prefillSkus=[]. Modal opens with mode='single', sku_code pre-filled.
existing_logic.openVendorTask: Sets prefillVendor=vendor string, prefillSkus=records array, prefillRecord=null. Modal opens with mode='vendor' with those SKUs pre-selected.
existing_logic.atRiskByVendor: Record<vendorName, InventoryRecord[]> — computed from all baseAtRisk records (not just visible), grouped by supplier_description. Passed to TaskModal as atRiskByVendor prop.

changes_required.change: Change row-level 'Task' buttons in the Attention Required table to open in vendor mode by default.
details: Replace all calls to openTaskForSku(r) in the Attention Required table rows with openVendorTask(r.supplier_description, baseAtRisk.filter(rec => rec.supplier_description === r.supplier_description)).
This means clicking Task on any at-risk SKU row now opens TaskModal in vendor mode, pre-selecting ALL at-risk SKUs from that vendor — not just the clicked one.
The user can still switch to single-SKU mode via the mode toggle inside TaskModal (the 'Single SKU / Vendor Order' tab switcher).
Do NOT remove or change the filter-bar Vendor Task button — it still has utility for quickly creating a task after filtering by a specific vendor.

changes_required.change: Add a Task button to each vendor header row in the Attention Required grouped table.
details: The vendor header row (the one with supplier name + SKU count + rec. order value summary) currently has no interactive buttons.

Add a <button onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); openVendorTask(group.vendor, baseAtRisk.filter(r => r.supplier_description === group.vendor)) }} className='btn-secondary text-[11px] py-1 px-2 flex items-center gap-1 ml-auto'><Plus size={11} /> Vendor Task</button> inside the vendor header row's flex container, to the right of the summary text.
Clicking it opens TaskModal in vendor mode with all at-risk SKUs from that vendor pre-selected.
e.stopPropagation() is required since the table row does not have a click handler, but keep it as defensive code.
The Plus icon is already imported in ActionCenter.

changes_required.change: Update openVendorTask calls to pass baseAtRisk filtered by vendor (already done for filter bar button, apply same pattern consistently).
details: The existing openVendorTask call in the filter bar already does: openVendorTask(arVendor, baseAtRisk.filter(r => r.supplier_description === arVendor)). This pattern is correct.
The new vendor header row Task button should use the same pattern: openVendorTask(group.vendor, baseAtRisk.filter(r => r.supplier_description === group.vendor)).
The row-level Task button (changed above) should also use: openVendorTask(r.supplier_description, baseAtRisk.filter(rec => rec.supplier_description === r.supplier_description)).
This ensures all vendor task creation paths pass the same complete at-risk SKU list for that vendor.

tasks.desired_outcome: Task creation from Action Center defaults to vendor-level orders. Clicking Task on any individual at-risk SKU row opens the modal in vendor mode, pre-loading all at-risk SKUs from that vendor. Each vendor header row also has its own Task button for one-click vendor task creation. Single-SKU mode remains accessible via the mode toggle inside the modal for edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the Task button on any individual at-risk SKU row opens TaskModal in 'Vendor Order' mode (not 'Single SKU' mode).
The vendor dropdown in the modal is pre-selected to the vendor of the clicked SKU.
The SKU list in the modal is pre-populated with ALL at-risk SKUs from that vendor, not just the clicked one.
The user can manually switch to 'Single SKU' mode via the mode toggle inside the modal.
Each vendor header row in the Attention Required grouped table shows a 'Vendor Task' button on the right side of the header.
Clicking the vendor header Task button opens TaskModal in vendor mode pre-populated with all at-risk SKUs for that vendor — identical behavior to the row-level Task button.
If a vendor has only 1 at-risk SKU, the modal still opens in vendor mode (single SKU in the multi-SKU list).
The filter bar 'Vendor Task' button (when a vendor filter is active) is unchanged and still works.
No regression in the Backorders or Overstock sections — their Snooze/Restore/task buttons are unaffected.

The task created in vendor mode stores the vendor's supplier_code in sku_code field and embeds the full SKU list in the description via buildVendorTaskDescription() — this existing behavior is unchanged.
out_of_scope: Storing multiple SKU codes in the database tasks.sku_code column — it remains a single varchar. Multi-SKU data is embedded in the description text.
Changes to the Backorders or Overstock task creation buttons — those call openTaskForSku() and should remain single-SKU mode (backorder/overstock tasks are typically per-SKU action items).
Changes to TaskModal.tsx internals — the modal's mode switching, vendor dropdown, and SKU selector logic are already correct.
Changes to TasksPage.tsx (kanban) — grouping/display of tasks is a separate concern.
implementation_notes: atRiskByVendor in ActionCenter is computed from baseAtRisk (which excludes snoozed items). When calling openVendorTask from a row, use baseAtRisk.filter() rather than atRiskByVendor[vendor] to remain consistent with what's visible.
The modal already receives atRiskByVendor as a prop, which powers the vendor dropdown. No change needed to TaskModal props.

The vendor header rows are rendered inside groupedAtRisk.map(). The Task button goes inside the existing <td colSpan={11}> element, inside the flex container that already has group.vendor name and summary stats.

implementation_order.rationale: Export is purely additive (new buttons, no logic changes) — lowest risk, highest value. Vendor view filter is self-contained in one file. Vendor tasks changes modify ActionCenter behavior and require careful testing of the modal pre-fill path.

regression_risk.TASK-EXPORT: Very low. Adding buttons with onClick handlers. No existing logic touched.
regression_risk.TASK-VV-FILTER: Low. Adds state and a new filter pass before getVendorSkuRows(). Existing sort and text search logic untouched. StatusBadges is already tested.
regression_risk.TASK-VENDOR-TASKS: Medium. Changes the behavior of row-level Task buttons in ActionCenter. The TaskModal vendor mode is already proven to work (used via filter bar and VendorView). Risk: if atRiskByVendor for a vendor is empty (e.g. filtered out by dismissals), the modal opens in vendor mode with an empty SKU list — this is handled by the existing 'Select at least one SKU' validation in TaskModal.
